name: VA & TAPS Collaboration (MOA)
whatItProvides: Details the long-standing formal relationship between TAPS and the VA, including the Memorandum of Agreement first entered in 2014 and expanded in 2023, allowing direct referrals for counseling and benefit audits.

name: DoD Survivors Forum
whatItProvides: TAPS works closely with the VA and DoD Survivors Forum as a central clearinghouse to coordinate government and private survivor support policies and identify program gaps.

name: Federal Agencies Inter-agency Map
whatItProvides: Advocacy projects and resource coordination maps involving the VA, DoD, Department of Education, Department of Labor, HHS, and state/local entities.

name: Legislative Advocacy & Policy Tracking
whatItProvides: Tracks active federal legislation affecting survivor benefits, including advocating to eliminate the remarriage penalty and expand educational entitlements.

name: Survivor VA Benefits Navigator
whatItProvides: Dedicated referral pipeline to help survivors identify, prepare, and apply for VA educational grants, burial allowances, Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC), and housing benefits.
